entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop evaluated for tolerance to the herbicide Imazethapyr.

entities.name: IMAZETHAPYR
entities.description: A post-emergence herbicide used to screen for tolerance in chickpea genotypes.

entities.name: HERBICIDE TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand the application of Imazethapyr.

entities.name: DAYS TO 50% FLOWERING
entities.description: The number of days required for 50% of the crop to flower.

entities.name: NDVI
entities.description: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index used to measure plant health and vigor.

entities.name: DAYS TO MATURITY
entities.description: The duration from planting until the crop reaches physiological maturity.

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of seed produced by the plant.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total biological mass produced by the plant.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of seed yield to total biomass.

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of 100 seeds, indicating seed size and quality.

entities.name: BRANCHED CHAIN AMINO ACIDS
entities.description: A group of amino acids including valine, leucine, and isoleucine.

entities.name: VALINE
entities.description: A specific branched-chain amino acid analyzed for content in chickpea.

entities.name: LEUCINE
entities.description: A specific branched-chain amino acid analyzed for content in chickpea.

entities.name: ISOLEUCINE
entities.description: A specific branched-chain amino acid analyzed for content in chickpea.

entities.name: TUKEY'S TEST
entities.description: A statistical method used for pairwise comparison of means to determine significant differences.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, providing the core collection of genotypes.

entities.name: GENOTYPE SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating multiple genotypes to identify those with specific traits.
